zh.Neuss, Rhineland, Prussia, German Empire: 海因里希_海普洛文次可萊斯 斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jan I van Loon-Chiny於大約1225年出生在比利時布拉班特時，他的父親Graaf Arnold IV van Loon24歲，母親Jeanne de Chiny21歲。他於大約1258年與Mathilde von Jülich結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1278年1月19日在他的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.Neuss, Rhineland, Prussia, German Empire: 海因里希_海普洛文次可萊斯 斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jan I van Loon-Chiny于大约1225年出生在比利时布拉班特时，他的父亲Graaf Arnold IV van Loon24岁，母亲Jeanne de Chiny21岁。他于大约1258年与Mathilde von Jülich结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1278年1月19日在他的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jan I van Loon-Chiny was born about 1225, in Brabant, Belgium, his father, Graaf Arnold IV van Loon, was 24 and his mother, Jeanne de Chiny, was 21. He married Mathilde von Jülich about 1258. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 19 January 1278, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jan I van Loon-Chiny est né environ 1225, à Brabant, Belgique, son père, Graaf Arnold IV van Loon, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Jeanne de Chiny, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde von Jülich environ 1258. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 19 janvier 1278, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jan I van Loon-Chiny um 1225 in Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Graaf Arnold IV van Loon 24 und seine Mutter Jeanne de Chiny 21. Er hat um 1258 Mathilde von Jülich geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 19. Januar 1278 im Alter von 54 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Loon-Chiny è nato il circa 1225 nel Brabante, Belgio, il suo padre, Graaf Arnold IV van Loon, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Jeanne de Chiny, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde von Jülich circa 1258. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 19 gennaio 1278, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan I van Loon-Chinyが約1225年にベルギーブラバントで生まれた時，父Graaf Arnold IV van Loonは24歳，母Jeanne de Chinyは21歳であった。約1258年にMathilde von Jülichと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1278年1月19日に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan I van Loon-Chiny(이)가 대략 1225년 벨기에 브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Graaf Arnold IV van Loon(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Jeanne de Chiny(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1258년 Mathilde von Jülich(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1278년 1월 19일 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Loon-Chiny nasceu aproximadamente 1225, em Brabante, Bélgica, seu pai, Graaf Arnold IV van Loon, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Jeanne de Chiny, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde von Jülich aproximadamente 1258. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 19 de janeiro de 1278, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jan I van Loon-Chiny родился около 1225 г. в местности брабант, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Graaf Arnold IV van Loon было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Jeanne de Chiny, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde von Jülich около 1258 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 19 января 1278 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jan I van Loon-Chiny nació aproximadamente 1225, en Brabante, Bélgica, su papá, Graaf Arnold IV van Loon, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Jeanne de Chiny, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Mathilde von Jülich aproximadamente 1258. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 19 de enero de 1278, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.
